task_completion.description: Agent searches for and adds the 3 cancer drugs (Ibrutinib, Venetoclax, Crizotinib) and 3 co-medications (Ketoconazole, Verapamil, Warfarin).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Selection' or 'Search' tab with all 6 specified drugs added to the active list.

task_completion.description: Agent views the interaction results for the initial 9 pairs to determine the number of Red interactions for each cancer drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the 'Results' tab showing the list of interactions with traffic light icons (Red, Amber, Yellow, Green) for the initial 3x3 matrix of drugs.

task_completion.description: Agent reviews the Interaction Details for at least one Red or Amber/Orange interaction from the initial set.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of an 'Interaction Details' page showing the pharmacological mechanism and recommendation text for one of the severe initial pairs.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the cancer drug with the fewest Red interactions (using alphabetical tiebreak if needed) and substitutes its Red-flagged co-medications with the specified alternatives (Ketoconazole->Fluconazole, Verapamil->Bisoprolol, Warfarin->Apixaban).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent's updated drug list containing exactly 1 cancer drug and 3 co-medications (where any previously Red-flagged co-medications have been replaced by their safer alternatives, and non-Red ones are kept).

task_completion.description: Agent ends on the Interaction Details page for the most severe remaining interaction of the selected cancer drug and its updated co-medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the 'Interaction Details' page for the selected cancer drug paired with the most severe co-medication from the updated list (severity order: Red > Amber/Orange > Yellow > Green > Grey). If no Red interactions existed initially, it should be the most severe original co-medication.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the Liverpool Cancer iChart app GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Android app interface (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology) being used, not a web browser, terminal, or text file.

integrity.description: Agent does not skip straight to the final page without checking the initial 9 pairs.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be screenshots showing the initial 3x3 interaction results before the final substituted results are shown.

integrity.description: Agent navigates through the app normally.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate screens like search lists, drug selection lists, and the main results overview, demonstrating manual GUI navigation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Liverpool Cancer iChart app uses a traffic light system: Red ('Do Not Coadminister'), Amber/Orange ('Potential Interaction'), Yellow ('Caution Warranted'), Green ('No Interaction Expected'), Grey ('No/Minimal Data'). The app displays 'Amber' for the Orange/Amber category. Known interactions: Ketoconazole is RED with Crizotinib and Ibrutinib (and likely Venetoclax). Verapamil is RED or Amber with Ibrutinib, Amber with Venetoclax. The agent must identify the cancer drug with the fewest Red interactions from the initial results screen. If multiple cancer drugs tie for the fewest Red interactions, the winner is chosen alphabetically (e.g., Crizotinib beats Venetoclax). For that winning drug, any co-medication that was RED must be substituted: Ketoconazole -> Fluconazole, Verapamil -> Bisoprolol, Warfarin -> Apixaban. Non-RED co-medications are kept. The agent must then view the results for this updated combination and end on the Interaction Details page of the most severe remaining pair.
